title: Add Excel Import/Export to Inventory Page

requirements.summary: Add Excel export button to Inventory page that downloads all inventory items as a well-formatted .xlsx file with proper column headers and numeric formatting
acceptanceCriteria: A clearly labeled 'Download Excel' button is visible on the Inventory page toolbar.
Clicking the button triggers a download of a .xlsx file (not .csv).
The downloaded file contains all current inventory items with columns: Name, Type, Purchase Price, Selling Price, Stock, Unit.
The file opens correctly in Excel 2007 and newer versions.
Column headers are bold and clearly formatted.
Numeric price and stock values are stored as numbers (not strings) in the spreadsheet.

file_operations.description: Add xlsx library (SheetJS) as a dependency to enable .xlsx file generation and parsing for Excel export and import functionality.

Add a 'Download Excel' button to the inventory page toolbar above the table. Implement an onClick handler that fetches all inventory items via the existing useInventoryItems hook, transforms the data into a structured format with columns (Name, Type, Purchase Price, Selling Price, Stock, Unit), generates a .xlsx file using the xlsx library with bold headers and proper numeric cell types, and triggers a browser download with filename 'inventory-export.xlsx'. Ensure Type field maps 'goods'/'service' to 'Goods'/'Service', stock shows the quantity for goods or 'N/A' for services, and prices are formatted as numbers without currency symbols in the spreadsheet.

requirements.summary: Add Excel import button to Inventory page that parses uploaded .xlsx files with support for both semicolon and comma separators, validates rows, and bulk-imports items to the backend
acceptanceCriteria: A clearly labeled 'Upload Excel' button is visible on the Inventory page toolbar.
Clicking the button opens a file picker that accepts .xlsx files.
The system correctly parses .xlsx files regardless of whether the regional Excel setting uses ; or , as separators.
Imported rows are validated: Name is required; Type must be 'goods' or 'service'; numeric fields (prices, stock) must be valid numbers.
Invalid rows are reported to the user with a clear error message indicating which rows failed and why.
Successfully imported items are added to the backend inventory and the inventory table refreshes automatically.
Duplicate items (by name) prompt the user to choose to skip or overwrite.

file_operations.description: Add an 'Upload Excel' button to the inventory page toolbar next to the Download Excel button. Implement an onClick handler that opens a file picker accepting .xlsx files. When a file is selected, read it using the xlsx library to parse the workbook. Extract the first sheet and convert rows to JSON, handling both semicolon and comma delimiters by detecting the cell content structure. Validate each row: ensure Name is non-empty, Type is 'Goods' or 'Service' (case-insensitive, map to 'goods'/'service'), Purchase Price and Selling Price are valid positive numbers, Stock is a valid non-negative number for goods (optional for services), and Unit is non-empty. Collect validation errors with row numbers. Check for duplicates by comparing lowercase names against existing inventory items fetched via useInventoryItems. If duplicates exist, show a confirmation dialog asking to skip or overwrite. Display validation errors in a dialog if any rows fail. For valid rows, call the addInventoryItem mutation from useQueries for each item with a generated ID (using Date.now() + index or similar), then invalidate and refetch the inventory query to refresh the table.

requirements.summary: Provide a downloadable Excel template file with pre-filled headers and an example row to guide users in preparing import data
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Download Template' link or button is available near the Upload Excel button.
The template .xlsx file contains bold headers: Name, Type, Purchase Price, Selling Price, Stock, Unit.
One example row is included demonstrating the expected format (e.g., 'Brake Pad, goods, 50000, 75000, 10, pcs').
The template file opens correctly in all Excel versions.

file_operations.description: Add a 'Download Template' link or button to the inventory page toolbar near the Upload Excel button. Implement an onClick handler that programmatically creates a .xlsx file using the xlsx library containing bold column headers (Name, Type, Purchase Price, Selling Price, Stock, Unit) in the first row and one example data row (e.g., 'Brake Pad', 'Goods', 50000, 75000, 10, 'pcs') with proper cell types (text for Name/Type/Unit, number for prices and stock). Apply bold formatting to the header row using xlsx cell styling. Trigger a browser download with filename 'inventory-template.xlsx'.
